title: clarkia amoena subsp. lindleyi
intro: Clarkia amoena subsp. lindleyi, also known as the pink godetia or fire pink, is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family. It is native to the western coast of North America, where it grows in coastal scrub and grasslands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Clarkia amoena subsp. lindleyi can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system.
